name: tuitter
readme_summary: tuitter is a terminal UI client for X (formerly Twitter) built with TypeScript. It allows users to authenticate with their X account and interact with content directly from their command-line interface.
primary_use_case: Interacting with X (Twitter) content and features directly from the terminal.
cover_image_prompt: A person sitting at a minimalist desk, looking at a sleek, glowing terminal screen. Instead of a typical browser interface, the screen displays a stylized, simplified representation of the X (Twitter) feed, with icons for tweets, likes, and retweets. The user's hands are on a keyboard, subtly interacting with the terminal. Floating around the terminal are abstract data streams and small, glowing icons representing tweets and user profiles. The overall mood is focused and efficient, suggesting powerful interaction within a confined digital space. The art style should be a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration with a cool color palette and subtle glowing effects.

name: caesium-app-web
description: A modern, privacy aware web app to compress images, all offline and within the browser.
readme_summary: Caesium Web Image Compressor is a privacy-focused, offline web application that compresses images directly in the browser using WebAssembly. It supports various image formats and compression types, ensuring no data is uploaded to external servers.
key_features: Runs entirely on your browser, no images are uploaded or processed on any external server
primary_use_case: Compressing images offline and in the browser while maintaining user privacy.
cover_image_prompt: A digital alchemist in a clean, minimalist workshop, holding a large, uncompressed digital image file represented as a glowing, pixelated orb. With a gentle gesture, the alchemist directs streams of light from a small, unobtrusive browser window interface towards the orb. The orb is visibly shrinking and becoming more compact, transforming into a smaller, perfectly formed digital image icon. Subtle data particles flow from the browser interface to the alchemist's hands. The background is a soft gradient, with the repository name 'Caesium' subtly etched into a workbench. The scene should be rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style, with a focus on clarity and a clean, modern aesthetic, emphasizing the transformation and privacy aspects.

name: zero_password_manager
description: Open-source self-hosted password manager built with Flutter. Store passwords and crypto seed phrases securely without cloud storage.
readme_summary: Zero Password Manager is an open-source, self-hosted password manager built with Flutter, prioritizing privacy and security. It allows users to store passwords and crypto seed phrases securely on their own servers, employing end-to-end encryption and zero-knowledge principles.
key_features: Support for Android, iOS, Web, and Desktop platforms
primary_use_case: To provide users with a secure, private, and self-controlled method for storing and managing sensitive credentials and seed phrases, eliminating reliance on third-party cloud services.
cover_image_prompt: A highly secure, transparent vault made of crystal, floating in a serene, private digital space. Inside the vault, digital keys and sensitive documents are neatly organized. A single, strong padlock on the vault is glowing, symbolizing robust security. Subtle streams of encrypted data flow into the vault from a user's personal device (represented by a stylized smartphone icon), while the vault itself is clearly anchored to a small, personal server icon, emphasizing self-hosting. The background is dark and uncluttered, focusing all attention on the vault and its secure contents. The overall mood is one of absolute privacy and control. Style: 3D isometric illustration, clean lines, soft glowing effects, deep blue and silver color palette.

name: rom
description: A browser-like runtime in Rust, built without Chromium. ROM composes an embedded JavaScript engine, browser-facing host objects, and a compatibility-driven runtime for deterministic web automation, surface emulation, and browser API research.
readme_summary: ROM is a Rust-based browser runtime designed to be lightweight and programmable, emulating browser APIs without relying on Chromium. It's ideal for deterministic web automation, surface emulation, and browser API research.
key_features: Deterministic probes, snapshots, and acceptance harnesses
primary_use_case: Deterministic web automation and browser API research
cover_image_prompt: A skilled artisan meticulously crafting a miniature, functional clockwork replica of a grand library. The artisan uses specialized tools to assemble tiny gears and circuits, representing the ROM runtime building browser APIs. Scrolls of code and data flow into the artisan's workspace, while the finished clockwork library emits a soft, organized glow. Small, subtle UI elements resembling browser developer tools are integrated into the scene. The overall mood is one of precision and controlled creation, set in a clean, modern workshop with focused lighting. The image should be in a 3D isometric illustration style, emphasizing detail and clarity.
